Welcome to the Lucypher app!

Lucypher

Lucypher is a web application that presents certain traditional cryptographic algorithms in Python code.

AES

1997 - The Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is a symmetric block cipher with 128 bits block size and key sizes of 128, 192 and 256 bits. Not a pure implementation is provided.

Caesars

50 BC - The Caesar cipher is a substitution monoalphabetic unilateral technique.

DES

1970 - The Data Encryption Standard (DES) is a block cipher invented in the early 1970s by IBM and the U.S. government (US patent 3,962,539). Not a pure implementation is provided.

Grille

1881 - The Grille cipher is a transposition technique.

Hill

1929 - The Hill cipher is a substitution polyalphabetic technique.

Lucifer

1971 - The Lucifer cipher is a product (substitution-transposition) technique. Not a pure implementation is provided.

Playfair

1854 - The Playfair cipher is a substitution monoalphabetic polygraphic technique.

Vigenère

16-th century - The Vigenère cipher is a substitution polyalphabetic based on a table technique.
